reflection.overall_reflection: The assessment felt too simplistic and lacked the technical depth I expect as an AI engineer working with agent workflows. The UI was underwhelming and failed to engage me as a power user.
usability_issues: Lack of clear progress indicators made it hard to gauge where I was in the assessment.
Buttons lacked visual feedback after selection, making it unclear if my choices were registered.
Labels for automation levels were truncated or incomplete, requiring me to read aria-labels for full understanding.
The interface assumed a general audience rather than catering to advanced users in agent orchestration.
reflection.nps_reason: I would not recommend this assessment to other AI engineers due to its lack of depth and poor usability for advanced users. The repetitive nature and oversimplified questions were frustrating and unengaging.
reflection.strongest_moment: The most frustrating moment was when the buttons for automation levels had incomplete labels, forcing me to infer meaning and read aria-labels for clarity.
reflection.share_reason: I wouldn't share this with my colleagues because it doesn't reflect the sophistication of our work and lacks meaningful insights for professionals in agent workflows.
reflection.accessibility_note: The page structure was confusing and lacked clear section labels or progress indicators, making it harder to navigate, especially for someone accustomed to advanced tools.

sus.sus_notes: The assessment felt overly simplistic and failed to engage with the depth of agent orchestration workflows I work with daily. The UI lacked nuance and feedback, making it frustrating for an advanced user like myself.
